design_philosophy.core_loop: Pilot one of three selectable Silver Hawks through horizontally-scrolling stages, destroying enemy formations and bosses while collecting power-ups to increase firepower and survivability
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (power-ups lost on death) balanced against pattern recognition in enemy waves and boss attack cycles
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a customizable fighter craft with multiple weapon configurations in an alien conflict across branching mission paths
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Three distinct Silver Hawk options (green/blue/red) create meaningful initial choice; branching stage structure (unlike previous Darius games) allows players to choose forward or up paths until reaching final stages L/N/O, offering quasi-replayability through route selection; intentional power-down mechanic on ship loss raises stakes

difficulty.lives_system: Multi-ship system; destroying formations, meteors, and cages provides extra ships via green power-ups
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint system returns player to stage checkpoint on ship loss; if lost during boss fight, returns to checkpoint before boss appears
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects one of three distinct Silver Hawks at start, affecting shot/bomb performance; R button allows switching between standard bombs and side laser guns mid-stage; no explicit difficulty modes documented

progression.upgrade_system: Three power-up types: red bubble (shot/bomb power-up), blue bubble (shield regenerate and level up), green bubble (bonus points or extra ship). Special power-up destroys all on-screen enemies or grants powerful laser beam. Power-ups obtained by: destroying complete enemy formations, destroying flashing meteors during meteor rain, destroying futuristic cages on floor/ceiling
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression unlocks via forward/up path selection until reaching stages L, N, or O (three distinct final bosses/endings)
replayability_hooks: Three selectable Silver Hawks with different characteristics
Branching stage map structure allowing forward or up path choices
Three distinct endings corresponding to final stages L, N, O
Extra ship availability encourages formation-destruction skill optimization

world_building_techniques: Alien planet setting established through stage progression and boss design
Three distinct Silver Hawk prototypes (including new red variant) imply technological advancement narrative
Enemy formations and environmental hazards suggest ongoing extraterrestrial conflict

boss_design.philosophy: Series-consistent design emphasizing recognizable patterns and phase-based attack cycles; three final bosses (stages L, N, O) provide distinct climactic encounters tied to route selection
notable_bosses: Stage L final boss (accessible via forward path route)
Stage N final boss (accessible via forward/up path combination)
Stage O final boss (accessible via forward/up path combination)

faction_indicators: Three Silver Hawk variants represent different military/scientific programs
Red prototype suggests cutting-edge faction advancement
Player choice of aircraft implies alignment with different command structures
danger_signals: Enemy formations increase in complexity across stages
Meteor rain sequences introduce environmental hazards
Boss encounters designed as skill gates with pattern-based vulnerabilities
environmental_storytelling: Futuristic cage structures on floors/ceilings suggest alien architecture/defense systems
Progression through alien environments establishes extraterrestrial setting
Enemy ship designs convey sci-fi conflict narrative
